earnings.summary: IBM beat Q1 FY2026 on every metric: revenue $15.92B (+9% YoY, beat +$300M), non-GAAP EPS $1.91 (+19%, beat +$0.10), FCF $2.22B — decade-best Q1. Software +11%, z17 mainframe +51%, GenAI book $12.5B+. Guidance maintained citing macro uncertainty.
earnings.execSummary: IBM delivered a clean beat across revenue, EPS, and free cash flow in Q1 FY2026. Software accelerated to +11% YoY, z17 mainframe surged +51%, and the GenAI book of business crossed $12.5B inception-to-date. FCF of $2.22B was the strongest Q1 in a decade. Management maintained rather than raised full-year guidance citing macro uncertainty. Rating: BUY · PT $250.
earnings.revenueGrowthAnalysis: Software at $7.05B (+11%) led growth. Infrastructure at $3.33B (+15%) powered by 51% surge in z17 IBM Z mainframe. Consulting at $5.27B (+4% reported, +1% CC) was the only mixed signal. EPS grew 19% YoY driven by SGA efficiencies and operating leverage.
earnings.segmentAnalysis: Software (44%): $7.05B +11% — Red Hat +13%, Automation +15%, Watsonx +7%. Infrastructure (21%): $3.33B +15% — IBM Z mainframe +51%. Consulting (33%): $5.27B +4% (+1% CC). Financing (1%): $220M +15%.
earnings.industryAnalysis: IBM targets regulated enterprises that cannot move to public cloud. Watsonx provides AI governance, Red Hat OpenShift provides hybrid infrastructure. GenAI book $12.5B+ converts to recurring licenses and consulting. z17 enables AI inference on-premise. Confluent adds real-time data streaming.
earnings.guidanceAnalysis: FY2026: CC revenue 5%+, Software 10%+ CC (raised), FCF $13.5B+, op margin +~1pt, Confluent dilution ~$600M. Guidance maintained despite beat — macro uncertainty cited.
mgmtPositive: CEO Krishna: We exceeded expectations for revenue, profitability and FCF, led by Software strength
GenAI book $12.5B+ — real revenue commitments converting to recurring licenses
Software guidance raised to 10%+ CC — reflects Confluent and Automation acceleration
FCF $2.22B strongest Q1 in decade — $13.5B+ FY2026 target intact
mgmtNegative: Guidance maintained not raised despite $300M beat — macro environment is fluid
Consulting +4% reported, just +1% CC — below acceleration needed
Confluent dilution ~$600M FY2026 EPS — near-term headwind
Infrastructure guided to decline low-single-digits FY despite Q1 +15%
bullPoints: GenAI book $12.5B+ — largest enterprise AI commitment outside hyperscalers
Software 10%+ CC compounding — mix-shift to high-margin recurring revenue
z17 super-cycle pacing ahead of z16 — AI inferencing on-premise extends cycle
FCF decade-best Q1 $2.22B — 3%+ dividend yield, 31st consecutive annual increase
bearPoints: Consulting +1% CC — structural weakness risk if no acceleration in H2
Confluent $11B acquisition — $600M EPS dilution, integration risk
Valuation 22-23x P/E — premium to 14x historical average
z17 cycle becomes headwind in H2 — infrastructure guided to decline FY
nearRisks: Q2 consulting below +3% would signal structural not cyclical weakness
z17 compare difficult in H2 as hardware ramp laps itself
Macro tariff uncertainty delaying enterprise consulting and AI projects
longRisks: Hyperscaler hybrid products targeting IBM regulated enterprise differentiation
Consulting commoditisation — GenAI automating labour-intensive work
Debt $66.4B post-Confluent — rising rates increase financing cost
earnings.valuation: IBM at ~$214 trades at 22-23x CY2026 non-GAAP EPS ~$10.80. FCF yield ~5.5%. Dividend yield ~3.0%. PT $250 base = 23x FY2026E EPS.
earnings.ptBullCase: $290. Software 12%+ FY2026. Consulting 5%+ H2. Confluent ahead of plan. EPS ~$11.50. 26x = $299.
earnings.ptBaseCase: $250. Software 10% on guide. Consulting 3-4%. FCF $13.5B+. EPS ~$10.80. 23x = $248.
earnings.ptBearCase: $175. Consulting stalls under 2%. Software misses. EPS $9.50. 18x = $171.
earnings.thesis: IBM is executing a credible transformation into a software-led hybrid cloud and enterprise AI platform. Q1 FY2026 confirmed: software 10%+, FCF decade-best, GenAI backlog $12.5B+. BUY · PT $250.

earnings.summary: NVIDIA delivered another blockbuster quarter. Revenue $44.1B (+69% YoY) beat consensus $42.8B by $1.3B. Non-GAAP EPS $0.96 (+72%) beat $0.91 by $0.05. Data Center $39.1B (+73% YoY). Non-GAAP gross margin 78.4% (+180bps). Q2 guidance $45.0B beat consensus $43.8B. CEO Huang: Blackwell demand is insane.
earnings.execSummary: NVIDIA reported a near-perfect Q1 FY2026. Data Center at $39.1B (+73% YoY) confirms the AI super-cycle intact. Blackwell ahead of schedule. Non-GAAP gross margin 78.4% expanded 180bps YoY. CUDA ecosystem with 4M+ developers creates irreplaceable switching costs. Rating: STRONG BUY · PT $1,400.
earnings.revenueGrowthAnalysis: Data Center $39.1B (+73%, 89% of revenue) dominates. Blackwell ramping across all hyperscalers simultaneously. Gaming $3.8B (+18%). Automotive $0.6B (+72%). Non-GAAP gross margin 78.4% confirms extraordinary pricing power.
earnings.segmentAnalysis: Data Center (89%): $39.1B +73% YoY. Gaming (9%): $3.8B +18%. Professional Viz (1%): $0.5B +45%. Automotive (1%): $0.6B +72%.
earnings.industryAnalysis: Hyperscaler combined 2026 capex $470B+. CUDA ecosystem — 4M+ developers, 650+ applications — creates switching costs taking years to overcome. AI inference market growing faster than training as models deploy at scale.
earnings.guidanceAnalysis: Q2 FY2026: Revenue $45.0B beats consensus $43.8B by $1.2B. Non-GAAP gross margin ~78.5%. Supply, not demand, remains the constraint. FY2026 annualised run-rate approaching $175B+.
mgmtPositive: CEO Huang: Blackwell demand is insane — every major cloud provider scaling simultaneously
Supply constrained not demand — backlog extends into FY2027
Q2 guidance beat consensus by 3% — strong demand visibility
CUDA 4M+ developers creates deep switching costs
mgmtNegative: China export restrictions limit TAM permanently — Huawei Ascend gaining share
Gross margin sustainability questioned at 78.4% peak
Custom silicon risk — TPU, Trainium threat growing over time
bullPoints: Data Center TAM expanding — $470B+ hyperscaler capex, every dollar flows through NVIDIA first
Blackwell ahead of schedule — supply constraint means demand is real not pull-forward
Gross margin 78.4% — highest in history, extraordinary pricing power
CUDA 4M+ developers, 650+ apps — multi-year switching cost moat
bearPoints: China export restrictions permanently limit ~10% of TAM
Valuation 35x+ forward P/E prices perfection — any miss violent
Custom silicon: Google, Amazon, Meta investing in proprietary AI accelerators
Top 5 hyperscalers ~87% of Data Center revenue — extreme concentration
nearRisks: Hyperscaler capex guidance reduction directly pressures revenue
Export control escalation — further restrictions on Blackwell impair TAM
TSMC CoWoS packaging issues would delay Blackwell shipments
longRisks: AI model efficiency improvements reducing GPU demand per capability unit
AMD ROCm gaining developer adoption over time
earnings.valuation: NVDA at ~$1,124 trades at ~35x FY2026E non-GAAP EPS $3.68. NTM EV/Revenue ~11.7x. PEG ~0.7x on 50%+ growth. Consensus: $1,350 avg PT, 22 buys, 4 holds.
earnings.ptBullCase: $1,800. FY2026 revenue $180B+, gross margin 80%+, EPS $4.80, 40x FY2026E.
earnings.ptBearCase: $700. Capex cycle inflects, revenue growth decelerates to 20%, EPS $3.00, 25x.
earnings.thesis: NVIDIA is the operating system of AI infrastructure. CUDA moat, Blackwell leadership, NVLink ecosystem take years to erode. PEG 0.7x on 50%+ growth is inexpensive. STRONG BUY · PT $1,400.

earnings.summary: Microsoft crushed estimates. Azure accelerated to +35% CC — fastest since 2022. Copilot M365 reached 30M paid seats. Operating margin 47.1% expanded despite heavy AI investment. Q4 guidance $68.1-69.1B exceeded consensus $67.4B.
earnings.execSummary: Microsoft delivered a strong beat across all segments. Azure reacceleration to 35% CC is the key positive surprise. Copilot M365 at 30M paid seats demonstrates real commercial adoption. Operating margin 47.1% expanding despite $18B/quarter AI capex. Rating: STRONG BUY · PT $510.
earnings.revenueGrowthAnalysis: Azure reaccelerating to 35% CC (from 31% in Q2) is the standout. AI services contributed ~12pp of Azure growth. M365 Commercial +13% driven by E5 suite upgrades and Copilot attachment.
earnings.segmentAnalysis: Intelligent Cloud (Azure): $26.8B +21% reported (+35% CC). Productivity (M365): $20.4B +12%. Gaming: $6.4B +8%. LinkedIn: $4.2B +9%.
earnings.industryAnalysis: Enterprise cloud migration accelerating not decelerating. AI is incremental to base cloud spend. Microsoft is best positioned with both cloud infrastructure (Azure) and AI application layer (Copilot, GitHub).
earnings.guidanceAnalysis: Q4 FY2026: Revenue $68.1-69.1B (vs consensus $67.4B). Azure 34-35% CC sustained. Operating margin ~47%.
mgmtPositive: CEO Nadella: Every layer of the stack is being transformed by AI — Azure OpenAI used by 65% of Fortune 500
Azure reacceleration to 35% CC — sustained pace expected
Copilot M365 30M paid seats — fastest enterprise software ramp in company history
FCF $24.1B — self-funding entire AI capex programme without dilution
mgmtNegative: EU antitrust scrutiny on M365/Teams bundling
bullPoints: Azure 35% CC with AI contributing 12pp — reacceleration is structural
Copilot M365 30M paid seats at $30/seat/month = $10.8B ARR run-rate
Operating leverage at 47.1% margin despite $18B/quarter AI investment
65% Fortune 500 on Azure OpenAI — switching costs compound
bearPoints: Valuation 31x FY26E P/E with 15% revenue growth — high expectations
OpenAI partnership complexity — billions invested in potential competitor
EU and DOJ scrutiny of cloud bundling could force changes
Capex $80B+ for 2026 — if AI revenue disappoints this becomes a problem
nearRisks: Azure below 30% CC in Q4 would signal AI momentum fading
Copilot churn rate — 30M seats but retention data unavailable
longRisks: Custom model proliferation reduces Azure OpenAI moat
AWS rebound eating into Azure market share gains
earnings.valuation: MSFT at $442 trades at 31x FY26E non-GAAP EPS $13.86. NTM EV/Revenue ~12x. FCF yield ~3.5%. PT $510 = 34x FY27E EPS $15.80.
earnings.ptBullCase: $620. Azure sustains 35%+ growth. Copilot reaches 20% of M365 base. Op margin 50%+.
earnings.ptBaseCase: $510. Azure sustains 32-35% CC. Copilot 60M seats by FY27. FY27E EPS $15.80 x 34x.
earnings.ptBearCase: $340. Azure decelerates to 20% CC. Copilot churn high. EPS $13.50 x 26x.
earnings.thesis: Microsoft owns both cloud infrastructure (Azure) AND the AI application layer (Copilot, GitHub) to monetise the AI wave end-to-end. STRONG BUY · PT $510.

earnings.summary: Apple beat on Services ($26.3B record, +13.8% YoY) and iPhone outperformed in India (+22% YoY). Apple Intelligence features accelerating upgrade cycle. Gross margin 46.9% near all-time highs. $110B buyback announced. China revenue -8% YoY remains the key headwind.
earnings.execSummary: Apple delivered a clean beat driven by record Services revenue ($26.3B, 75%+ gross margin) and better-than-expected iPhone in India. Apple Intelligence is beginning to pull forward the upgrade cycle. Gross margin 46.9% demonstrates platform pricing power. Rating: BUY · PT $248.
earnings.revenueGrowthAnalysis: Services at $26.3B (+13.8% YoY) approaching 28% of revenue with 75%+ gross margins. iPhone $69.1B (+2.1%) outperformed driven by India +22%. China -8% drag.
earnings.segmentAnalysis: iPhone (72%): $69.1B +2.1% — US and India strong, China -8%. Services (28%): $26.3B +13.8% record. Mac: $7.9B +6.2%. iPad: $6.0B +8.1%. Wearables: $12.1B -3.1%.
earnings.industryAnalysis: Global smartphone market growing 3-5% annually. Services is the structural growth driver — 2.2B+ device installed base creates recurring monetisation. Apple Intelligence is the near-term catalyst for the next major iPhone upgrade cycle.
earnings.guidanceAnalysis: Q2 FY2026: Revenue $94-98B (vs consensus $96B — in-line). Gross margin 46.5-47.5%. Services growth ~13-14% YoY. $110B buyback — largest in company history.
mgmtPositive: CEO Cook: Apple Intelligence generating significant interest in iPhone upgrades
Services $26.3B record — never had stronger engagement with Apple ecosystem
India iPhone +22% YoY — one of fastest growing markets globally
$110B buyback — largest single announcement in Apple history
mgmtNegative: China -8% YoY — acknowledged continued challenges in Greater China
Vision Pro below sales trajectory — still in early adopter phase
bullPoints: Services $26.3B record with 75%+ gross margin — structural gross margin expansion engine
Apple Intelligence upgrade cycle: 1.3B iPhone users on pre-AI models — 10% upgrading = 130M unit tailwind
India: 1.4B population, under 5% iPhone penetration — decade-long secular growth opportunity
$110B buyback + dividend: ~4% total capital return yield provides meaningful floor
bearPoints: China -8% YoY — Huawei market share recovery is real and ongoing
iPhone unit growth +2.1% — hardware near-zero, bull case depends on Services and ASP
App Store antitrust risk — EU DMA forced sideloading, US DOJ case could force similar changes
Valuation 28x forward P/E for 4% revenue growth — expensive on growth-adjusted basis
nearRisks: China further deterioration directly impairs $15-20B annual revenue
Apple Intelligence adoption disappoints — if upgrade cycle does not materialise
App Store regulatory enforcement reduces Services margin
longRisks: Structural China challenge — Huawei premium smartphone share shift permanent
Services antitrust forced open access would reduce App Store commissions
Apple Intelligence competitive parity — Google Gemini, Samsung Galaxy AI catching up
earnings.valuation: AAPL at $209 trades at 28x FY26E EPS $7.48. NTM P/E 28x. EV/Sales ~8x. FCF yield ~3.6%. PT $248 = 30x FY27E EPS $8.20.
earnings.ptBullCase: $310. iPhone units +12% FY2027. Services grows 18%+. Gross margin 49%+. EPS $9.50 x 35x FY2027.
earnings.ptBaseCase: $248. Services 13-14%. iPhone +4-5% FY2027. Gross margin ~47%. EPS $8.20 x 30x premium.
earnings.ptBearCase: $160. China deteriorates. iPhone flat. App Store forced open. Services margin 65%. EPS $6.50 x 26x.
earnings.thesis: Apple Services flywheel at 13%+ growth, Apple Intelligence as next iPhone upgrade cycle catalyst, India as decade-long expansion. Franchise quality justifies premium multiple. BUY · PT $248.

earnings.summary: Amazon delivered strong Q1 2026. AWS reaccelerated to +21% growth. Operating income hit a record $18.4B (11.8% margin). Advertising +19%. EPS $1.59 grew 61% YoY. Q2 operating income guided $13.0-17.5B — wide range reflecting capex timing.
earnings.execSummary: Amazon Q1 2026 strong across the board: AWS +21% (reaccelerating), Advertising +19%, record operating margin 11.8%. EPS grew 61% YoY. Rating: BUY · PT $245.
earnings.revenueGrowthAnalysis: AWS reacceleration to 21% (from 18% in Q4 2025) is the key positive. Advertising $13.8B (+19%) is the highest-margin business. North America retail margin expansion from fulfillment automation.
earnings.segmentAnalysis: AWS (19% of rev): $29.3B +21% YoY. Advertising (9%): $13.8B +19%. North America Retail (60%): $92.9B +10%. International (22%): $34.0B +9%.
earnings.industryAnalysis: Cloud hyperscaler market growing 20-25% annually, AI incremental to base workloads. AWS has 31% market share. Amazon Bedrock provides multi-model AI positioning. Trainium reduces GPU dependency.
earnings.guidanceAnalysis: Q2 2026: Revenue $159-164B (vs consensus $160.4B — in-line). Operating income $13.0-17.5B wide range. $105B capex planned for 2026.
mgmtPositive: CEO Jassy: AWS demand strong and broad-based — every enterprise in some phase of AI adoption
AWS +21% — AI workloads becoming meaningful contributor to growth
Operating margin 11.8% — record for Amazon, demonstrating retail leverage
Advertising $13.8B +19% — Prime Video ads just launched
mgmtNegative: Q2 operating income guided $13-17.5B — very wide range signals uncertainty
Retail competition from Temu, Shein intensifying
bullPoints: AWS +21% reaccelerating with AI as incremental workload
Advertising 19% growth — Prime Video ads year-one revenue with multi-year runway
Record operating margin 11.8% — retail at 8%+ margins creates enormous upside
Bedrock multi-model AI platform — positioning among Claude, GPT, Llama
bearPoints: $105B 2026 capex — returns materialise in 2-3 years, FCF substantially negative
AWS +21% strong but below Azure +35% CC
Wide Q2 operating income range $13-17.5B — low end implies significant compression
International still loss-making despite improvement
nearRisks: Q2 operating income below $13B would confirm margin investment weighing heavily
AWS growth deceleration to 18% would signal AI workload contribution one-time
longRisks: Regulatory antitrust scrutiny on AWS bundling
Retail margin ceiling at 8-10% — future growth must come from AWS and advertising
earnings.valuation: AMZN at $213 trades at ~34x FY2026E EPS $6.50. EV/EBITDA ~22x. Sum-of-parts: AWS $150B+ revenue at 35%+ margins by 2028. PT $245.
earnings.ptBullCase: $310. AWS reaches 25%+ sustained. Advertising hits $20B FY2027. Retail margins 9%+.
earnings.ptBaseCase: $245. AWS sustains 20-22%. Advertising 15-18%. FY2027E EPS $8.50 x 30x.
earnings.ptBearCase: $145. AWS decelerates to 15%. Retail fails to hold margin. EPS $4.50 x 32x.
earnings.thesis: Amazon has three high-quality compounding engines: AWS, Advertising, and Retail. BUY · PT $245.

technical_analysis.indicators: RSI 58, MACD bullish cross, Volume 1.8x avg, Above 200MA
technical_analysis.quote: z17 mainframe super-cycle + Software 10%+ creates a durable multi-year earnings floor
technical_analysis.analysis: IBM has broken out of a multi-month consolidation range following the Q1 FY2026 earnings beat. The z17 mainframe cycle is pacing 51% ahead YoY. Software at 44% of revenue growing 10%+ CC provides structural growth. The post-earnings -6% pullback to $214 offers better risk/reward than pre-earnings. Key level to hold: $205 (50-day MA).
technical_analysis.buy_reasons:
z17 mainframe super-cycle +51% YoY — pacing ahead of z16
Software 10%+ CC — mix-shift to high-margin recurring revenue
GenAI book $12.5B+ — converts to recurring software and consulting
FCF $2.22B decade-best Q1 — $13.5B+ FY2026 well-supported
Post-earnings pullback -6% offers better entry at $214
technical_analysis.sell_reasons:
Consulting only +1% CC — weakness risk in H2 2026
Confluent $11B acquisition — $600M EPS dilution FY2026
Valuation 22-23x P/E — premium to 14x historical average
z17 cycle headwind builds in H2 as hardware laps itself

articles.title: Marvell Technology — Custom Silicon $10B+ & Optical Interconnect Leadership: Full Equity Research
articles.excerpt: Marvell has completed its transformation into a pure-play AI infrastructure platform. Custom silicon XPU at $10B+ FY29 target anchored in won design commitments, optical interconnect growing >70% in FY27, and NVLink Fusion partnership with NVIDIA. We build a 5-year model and assess whether 56x NTM P/E is justified.
articles.author: Balaji N, CFP

articles.title: IBM — Hybrid Cloud & AI Platform: Full Equity Research Report
articles.excerpt: IBM's transformation from legacy IT giant to software-led hybrid cloud and AI platform is underway. Software at 44% of revenue, growing 10%+ CC. GenAI book of business $12.5B+. z17 mainframe super-cycle. We analyse the business quality, competitive moat, 5-year revenue model, and whether the 22x P/E premium is justified.
articles.author: Balaji N, CFP

articles.title: NVIDIA — The Operating System of AI Infrastructure: Bull Case, Bear Case & Valuation
articles.excerpt: NVIDIA is not a semiconductor company — it is the operating system of AI infrastructure. CUDA ecosystem with 4M+ developers, Blackwell architecture, and NVLink fabric create a moat that takes years to replicate. We model three scenarios for the stock through FY2028 and explain why PEG 0.7x on 50%+ growth is inexpensive.
articles.author: Balaji N, CFP

articles.title: Microsoft — Azure + Copilot: Modelling the AI Monetisation Flywheel Through FY2028
articles.excerpt: Microsoft owns both the cloud infrastructure layer (Azure, 35% CC growth) and the AI application layer (Copilot M365 at 30M paid seats, GitHub Copilot at 2M+ enterprise). We model the compounding effect and build a sum-of-parts valuation that supports our $510 base target.
articles.author: Balaji N, CFP

articles.title: Apple — Services Flywheel & Apple Intelligence: The Path to 50% Gross Margin
articles.excerpt: Apple Services at $26.3B with 75%+ gross margins is the structural story behind the stock. We model how Apple Intelligence accelerates the iPhone upgrade cycle, India drives the next decade of hardware growth, and the blended gross margin path to 50% by 2028. Is 28x P/E justified?
articles.author: Balaji N, CFP

articles.title: Amazon — Three Compounding Engines: AWS, Advertising & Retail Margin Expansion
articles.excerpt: Amazon is not a retailer — it is three high-quality compounding businesses sharing a logistics backbone. AWS at 21% growth, Advertising at 19%, and retail margins expanding to record 11.8% operating margin. We model the $105B capex supercycle and when FCF recovers.
articles.author: Balaji N, CFP
